title: adicea ovalis
intro: Adicea ovalis, commonly known as Indian pennywort, is a perennial plant native to Asia, known for its medicinal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as diabetes, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its numerous health benefits, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Its therapeutic actions include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, which contribute to its ability to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Adicea ovalis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Adicea ovalis can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues. Ingesting the plant's toxic compounds can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while excessive topical application can lead to skin burns and lesions. Additionally, misuse of Adicea ovalis may result in kidney damage and liver toxicity, particularly when combined with other medications or substances. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxic compounds can also lead to more severe health complications, including organ failure and even death.
